title: Smoky Asian Broccoli Stir-fry
description: A rich and dense one-pot dish featuring broccoli with a smoky flavor profile that's low in fat but loaded with nutrients.
instructions: Clean and cut the broccoli into florets.
Heat oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic, red pepper flakes, and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add broccoli to the pan, tossing frequently to prevent sticking. Cook until slightly softened, about 5 minutes.
If using fish sauce, add it now and stir well. Pour broth over broccoli mixture and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for an additional 3-4 minutes or until all liquid is absorbed and broccoli is tender but still crisp.
Garnish with fresh herbs like cilantro before serving.
